hero.desc: Calm your skin, sleep better, and stop the flare cycle in Kuala Lumpur.
hero.sleepDesc: When eczema flares, it steals sleep, confidence, and comfort.
hero.doctorDesc: MOH-registered clinic with diagnosis-first approach in KLCC.

trust.t2Desc: LG 10, Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang

whyKL.desc: Malaysia's climate and lifestyle create a perfect storm for eczema
whyKL.trigger1Desc: Sweat can sting cracked skin and trigger itching
whyKL.trigger2Desc: Temperature swings stress your skin barrier
whyKL.trigger3Desc: Hot water strips natural protective oils

symptoms.s1: Intense itching, often before you see a rash
symptoms.s3: Red or darker patches (brown, purple, or grey)
symptoms.s4: Small bumps, oozing, or crusting during flares

treatments.desc: Evidence-based care tailored to your skin's needs
treatments.t1Desc: The foundation of eczema care. Moisturise every day, even when skin looks calm.
treatments.t2Desc: For active flares. Topical corticosteroids and calcineurin inhibitors when needed.
treatments.t3Desc: Cold compresses, night routine adjustments, and habit strategies.

routine.desc: Simple, sustainable steps that fit your daily rhythm
routine.midday2: Reapply moisturiser if skin feels tight

faq.q1: Will eczema go away without treatment?
faq.a1: Some mild cases improve, but eczema often flares and returns. Management and prevention are key.
faq.a2: No. Atopic dermatitis is not contagious. You cannot spread it by touch.
faq.q3: Why is eczema worse at night?
faq.a3: Many people feel itch more at night due to routine changes, body temperature shifts, and subconscious scratching.
faq.q4: Are steroid creams safe for eczema?
faq.a4: They are widely used and safe when used correctly with proper medical guidance on strength, area, and duration.

cta.title: Stop guessing with random creams
cta.desc: Visit Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur for a doctor-led skin assessment and a plan that fits real KL life
cta.bookBtn: Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, near KLCC
